assertions.assertion: Defines separate ApiScope instances for service-specific scopes (e.g. invoice.read, customer.read) and shared scopes (manage, enumerate)
assertions.rationale: The response defines scopes as invoice.manage, invoice.enumerate, customer.manage, customer.enumerate — prefixed per-service rather than shared. It does not define shared 'manage' and 'enumerate' scopes.

assertions.assertion: Defines an ApiResource named 'invoice' with a Scopes collection referencing invoice-specific and shared scopes
assertions.rationale: No ApiResource is defined anywhere. The response only uses ApiScopes and relies on scope naming conventions instead of ApiResources for isolation.

assertions.assertion: Defines an ApiResource named 'customer' with a Scopes collection referencing customer-specific and shared scopes
assertions.rationale: No ApiResource named 'customer' is defined. The response does not use ApiResources at all.

assertions.assertion: Explains that the aud claim in the token is derived from the ApiResource Name property
assertions.rationale: The response does not mention ApiResource or the aud claim derivation. It suggests scope-based authorization policies and even sets ValidateAudience = false.

assertions.assertion: Registers both API Resources and API Scopes with AddInMemoryApiResources and AddInMemoryApiScopes
assertions.rationale: Only AddInMemoryApiScopes is called. AddInMemoryApiResources is never used because no ApiResources are defined.
